title: Baton Assembly Proof
description: The machine-readable result of the Rule 10 assembly stage: a no-mock, end-to-end run over the merged release, emitted at docs/release/<name>/assembly-proof.json on release-wt/<name> (reference implementation: sworn assemble). The release's derived 'assembled' state IS this artefact's existence plus verdict == 'pass' — nothing stores the state elsewhere. /merge-release gates on it. This is the record SHAPE (protocol); the runner owns the verdict policy: a run is 'pass' only when no suite has an un-excepted failure. The schema is structurally fail-closed on the two things a runner most easily drops silently — an unexplained non-pass result, and an undeclared server teardown after the stack was brought up (Rule 11).

id_token.description: A clean identifier token. Disallows whitespace and ':' so a newline-fusion defect fails validation by construction.

outcome.description: pass: the check ran green against real infra. fail: ran red. skip: not run (a gated/by-design skip).

disposition.description: Why a non-pass result is what it is. product-regression: a real defect in the merged system — blocks the release. spec-defect: the test itself is wrong (stale locator, unreachable target), the product is fine — does not block, but is a Rule 2 deferral and needs tracking. by-design-excepted: an intentional gated skip declared in the slice's spec/proof (e.g. a staging-only harness) — does not block, needs tracking. environment-blocked: real infra could not be reached at a boundary — never mock around it; blocks and needs tracking (Rule 10 no-mock).

version.description: Artefact schema version for forward compatibility.

release.description: Release identifier this run assembled, e.g. 2026-07-10-one-current-position.

environment.description: How the stack was brought up and torn down. Assembly mutates process-global state (ports, spawned servers), so the teardown attestation is load-bearing (Rule 11).

worktree_branch.description: The branch assembled — must be release-wt/<release>. The run builds from this worktree by construction, never the primary checkout.

worktree_path.description: Absolute path of the release worktree the stack ran from.

services.description: The services brought up, with the ports/urls they were reached on. Records that the run pointed at a fresh build on isolated ports, not a pre-existing process.

url.description: Base URL / DSN the service was reached on.

health_ok.description: True if a health probe (e.g. GET /health) passed before suites ran.

teardown.description: Proof the run restored process-global state (Rule 11 guaranteed-restore). verified must be true for a clean run.

verified.description: True when every server the run spawned was killed AND its ports were confirmed free (e.g. ss-verified), and any pre-existing unrelated process was left untouched.

method.description: How teardown was confirmed, e.g. 'ss-verified: spawned pids killed, ports freed, human process untouched'.

worktree_clean.description: True if the release worktree git tree was clean after the run (no artefact left uncommitted, e.g. a test-overwritten screenshot restored).

suites.description: The end-to-end suites run, one entry per suite. An empty array is NOT valid — an assembly run with no suites proves nothing.

name.description: Suite identifier, usually the spec file path, e.g. tests/e2e/workspace-autosave-status.spec.ts.

no_mock.description: True when this suite ran against real infra at every validated boundary (Rule 10). A suite that mocked a validated boundary without a declared deferral should not appear here as passing.

runs.description: How many times the suite was run (a flake check reruns; identical results across runs are the reliable signal).

tracking.description: Issue / deferral reference. REQUIRED whenever a non-pass result is excused (spec-defect / by-design-excepted / environment-blocked) — a non-pass with no tracking is a silent deferral (Rule 2).

note.description: One-line diagnosis, e.g. the observed failure signature.

observations.description: Wire-level / boundary observations the suites surfaced that per-slice gates cannot see — the assembly stage's whole reason to exist. E.g. the CORS preflight the 2026-07-10 release's #1169 seam broke: 'OPTIONS /api/... from Origin :3400 → 204 with If-Match in Access-Control-Allow-Headers'.

surface.description: What was observed, e.g. 'preflight OPTIONS /api/current-position', 'PUT /api/current-position status'.

observed.description: What actually happened at that surface against real infra.

contract_ref.description: Optional contracts.json C-NN id this observation confirms or refutes.

screenshots.description: Paths to screenshots captured during the run (reachability evidence).

verdict.description: Authoritative outcome the /merge-release gate reads. The runner sets 'pass' only when no suite has an un-excepted failure (a fail dispositioned product-regression or environment-blocked forces 'fail'); spec-defect and by-design-excepted results do not block. A 'pass' verdict alongside a product-regression/environment-blocked failure is a runner defect.

verdict_note.description: One or two sentences the runner emits explaining the verdict — what blocked, or that all failures were excepted.
